what_it_does: Defines a 24x24 stroke-only SVG route icon with two endpoint circles, a connecting route path, and two short horizontal marks.
observations: Root SVG uses viewBox 0 0 24 24, fill="none", stroke="currentColor", stroke-width="1.5", rounded caps, and rounded joins.
Two circles mark points at (5,7) and (19,17), each with radius 2.
A routed path runs from the left circle through a horizontal segment, curve, vertical segment, and curve toward the right circle.
A second path draws two short horizontal marks from x=13 to x=15 at y=5 and y=9.
standouts: Uses currentColor so the icon inherits text or parent color.
Combines endpoint circles with a bent connector to communicate routing.
conventions: Follows a compact inline SVG icon pattern.
Uses stroke-only geometry with rounded line treatment.
Does not include IDs, classes, embedded styles, or accessibility text.
concerns: No title or aria metadata is present if this SVG is used standalone.
notes: A simple route/navigation icon asset built from circles and stroked paths.
questions: Is accessibility labeling expected to be supplied by the consuming component?
purpose_vs_actual: The filename suggests a route icon, and the SVG depicts a route between two points.
intention_signals: It appears intended to provide a reusable route icon that can adapt to surrounding color through currentColor.
contribution: Contributes a compact, color-inheriting route glyph with endpoint markers and a bent connector suitable for a design-system icon set.
summary_for_embedding: This is a 24x24 currentColor SVG route icon made of two endpoint circles, a bent connecting path, and two small horizontal marks.
intention_for_embedding: It was for representing routing, navigation, or path movement as a reusable stroke-based icon asset.
